more.title: South Africa and Zambia Move to Shield Economies from Global Fuel Price Shock
more.description: JOHANNESBURG / LUSAKA — Governments in South Africa and Zambia have rolled out emergency policy measures to cushion consumers and stabilise their economies as global oil prices surge following escalating tensions involving Iran, Israel and the United States. The coordinated, though structurally different, responses underscore the growing vulnerability of oil-importing economies to external energy shocks, [...] The post South Africa and Zambia Move to Shield Economies from Global Fuel Price Shock appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Filling stations across South Africa run out of petrol and diesel
more.description: Many filling stations across South Africa have run out of petrol and diesel ahead of the big price increases on 1 April 2026. more.title: Zimbabwe Grapples with Deepening Public Transport Crisis as Fares Surge by 70%
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe is facing a worsening public transport crisis after fares surged by as much as 70 percent, leaving thousands of commuters stranded and intensifying the cost-of-living pressures across urban centres. The sharp increase, driven largely by rising fuel costs, has disrupted daily commuting patterns, with transport operators rapidly adjusting fares to preserve already [...] The post Zimbabwe Grapples with Deepening Public Transport Crisis as Fares Surge by 70% appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Zimbabwe Targets Low Inflation as Anchor for Economic Recovery and Investment Growth
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe’s Treasury has set out an ambitious macroeconomic framework aimed at entrenching single-digit inflation, signalling a strategic shift from chronic instability towards a more predictable and investment-friendly economic environment. Authorities are positioning low and stable inflation as the foundation for long-term economic expansion, capital formation, and renewed foreign direct investment inflows.
